name: La Hoya de Buñol and Serra Perenxisa Loop from Picassent
name_original: La Hoya de Buñol y la Serra Perenxisa — Circular desde Picassent
description: This difficult 51.1-mile road cycling route from Picassent offers challenging climbs and varied scenery through the Serra Perenxisa.
region_intro: Road cycling routes Picassent offer diverse landscapes, from flat sections to challenging climbs, within the Valencian metropolitan area. The region features mostly paved surfaces, with some routes including unpaved segments. Cyclists can navigate quiet secondary roads that pass through picturesque villages, set against a Mediterranean backdrop. This variety provides options for different fitness levels and cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Picassent The most popular road cycling route is La Hoya de Buñol and Serra Perenxisa Loop from Picassent, a 51.1 miles (82.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ribera del Xúquer and Albufera Loop from Picassent, a difficult 62.2 miles (100.1 km) path. This route traverses flatter sections near the Albufera Natural Park, offering a different cycling experience. Local road cyclists also love the Venta Cabrera – Balcón de Montroy Pass loop from Picassent, a 45.6 miles (73.4 km) trail leading through hilly landscapes and the Balcón de Montroy Pass, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Picassent is defined by Mediterranean landscapes, picturesque villages, and quiet secondary roads. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easier rides to routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Picassent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 6,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Picassent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Picassent?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available around Picassent, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Picassent?
A: The region around Picassent offers diverse terrain, from flat sections ideal for speed to challenging climbs that test endurance. Routes often feature mostly paved surfaces, but some may include unpaved segments. You'll cycle through Mediterranean landscapes, picturesque villages, and quiet secondary roads.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Picassent?
A: Yes, Picassent offers several easier road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically have less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable experience for all skill levels.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes near Picassent?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Picassent has several difficult routes with significant elevation gains. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e841365350/la-hoya-de-bunol-and-serra-perenxisa-loop-from-picassent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hoya de Buñol and Serra Perenxisa Loop from Picassent&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 82 km with substantial climbing through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Picassent area?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Picassent are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e841365350/la-hoya-de-bunol-and-serra-perenxisa-loop-from-picassent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hoya de Buñol and Serra Perenxisa Loop from Picassent&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40019147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Venta Cabrera – Balcón de Montroy Pass loop from Picassent&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling around Picassent?
A: While cycling, you can explore various natural attractions. The region features natural sites like "La Cueva del Águila" and "La Cueva de Moneders" (caves), and springs such as "La Fuente de Ninyerola" and "La fuente de l'Omet." Many routes also pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique landscapes.

Q: Can I cycle near the Albufera Natural Park?
A: Yes, you can cycle near the Albufera Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e858878576/ribera-del-xuquer-and-albufera-loop-from-picassent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Xúquer and Albufera Loop from Picassent&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 100 km route that traverses flatter sections near the park, allowing you to experience its extensive rice fields and serene lagoons. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2439037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic routes with good views?
A: Absolutely. The road cycling routes around Picassent are known for their scenic beauty, often passing through Mediterranean landscapes and picturesque villages. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pujol Pond – Albufera Viewpoint loop from Omet&lt;/a&gt; offer beautiful vistas, including views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2439037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Picassent?
A: Picassent, being a town within the Valencian metropolitan area, offers various public parking options. While specific cyclist-dedicated parking isn't highlighted, you can typically find parking in and around the town center or near popular route starting points.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Picassent?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes Picassent suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. However, professional cyclists train in the wider Valencia region year-round, indicating good conditions are often available.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Picassent?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of flat and challenging sections, and the opportunity to cycle through picturesque villages on quiet secondary roads, all set against a beautiful Mediterranean backdrop.
tips: Panoramic point of the Serra Perenxisa where you can enjoy very nice views towards Valencia and the Albufera, getting to see the sea on a day with good visibility.
This stretch of road is quite demanding despite its short length. On your way you can enjoy very nice views of both the city of Buñol and the Hoya de Buñol with very picturesque landscapes.

faqs.question: How challenging is the road cycling route from Picassent?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and is a hard road ride. It covers 82.3 kilometers with an elevation gain of 910 meters, making it suitable for cyclists with a very good fitness level. Expect significant climbs and demanding sections.

faqs.question: How long should I expect to spend cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated completion time for this 82.3-kilometer circular route is approximately 3 hours and 55 minutes. However, this is for experienced cyclists with a very good fitness level, so plan accordingly based on your pace and any stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on mostly well-paved surfaces. However, given its difficulty and the hilly nature of the region, some segments may include unpaved and difficult sections. You will encounter significant ascents and descents.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route in Picassent?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the starting point in Picassent are not provided, as a circular route, you would typically look for public parking options within Picassent itself to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Can I reach the starting point in Picassent by public transport?
faqs.answer: Picassent is accessible by public transport, including metro lines from Valencia. You can plan your journey to the town center to begin your cycling adventure from there.

faqs.question: What scenic views or highlights can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse landscapes. You can enjoy a fantastic View of Valencia and Albufera from Serra Perenxisa. You will also pass the challenging Monte de la Cruz Pass – Río Juanes Climb. The wider region features varied scenery, from riverine areas to more arid, hilly zones.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the demanding nature of the route and the climate of the Valencia region, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle. Temperatures are milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Serra Perenxisa Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Paraje Natural Municipal Serra Perenxisa. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within natural parks in this region, but always respect local regulations and signage regarding access and conservation.

faqs.question: Is this circular road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in many natural areas, bringing a dog on a demanding 82.3 km road bike ride is not recommended due to the distance, speed, and potential for traffic. It is not considered a dog-friendly activity.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths, including sections of the E-1 Urbanització El Pantà - Urbanització Calicanto, e13 Enllaç E5 amb E4, e5 Enllaç E1 amb E1 pel Camí de la Serra, and the Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Valencia.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling route?
faqs.answer: For this difficult and long road cycling route, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire for the weather, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device or a printed map is also highly recommended.
